title: Dialog
description: A modal dialog overlays the page to focus the user on a single task or decision.
whenToUse: For confirmations of destructive actions. For short forms that need focus. For critical information requiring acknowledgement.
whenNotToUse: For non-blocking notifications (use Notification). For multi-step flows (use a dedicated page or wizard).
acceptanceCriteria: Uses role="dialog" with aria-modal="true" and aria-labelledby pointing at the title element
Backdrop renders behind the dialog and intercepts pointer events on the page below
On open, focus moves into the dialog; on close, focus returns to the element that triggered open
Tab and Shift+Tab cycle focus inside the dialog (focus trap) while open
Escape closes the dialog when dismissible=true and emits close with reason='escape'
Backdrop click closes the dialog when dismissible=true and emits close with reason='backdrop'
Close button is the first focusable element by default; consumer can override via slot
1- to 4-slot body layouts render with consistent spacing
Primary/secondary/tertiary footer actions render at the documented positions and emit close with the matching reason
Body of the dialog is scrollable when content overflows; the title bar and footer remain fixed
When loading=true on the primary action, Escape and backdrop close are disabled

props.name: open
props.description: Controls visibility. When true, the backdrop renders and focus moves into the dialog.

props.name: title
props.description: Dialog title — surfaced as the accessible name via aria-labelledby

props.name: dismissible
props.description: Allows closing via the X button, Escape, and backdrop click

props.name: tertiaryAction
props.description: Optional third footer action; aligned to the left

events.name: open
events.description: Fires after the dialog is rendered and focus has moved inside

events.name: close
events.description: Fires when the dialog is dismissed

events.name: confirm
events.description: Convenience event when the primary action fires

events.name: cancel
events.description: Convenience event when secondary or escape closes the dialog

slots.name: header
slots.description: Override the title bar; replaces title prop when present

slots.name: body
slots.description: 1 to 4 content slots stacked vertically; the dialog supports up to four for short forms

slots.name: footer
slots.description: Override the action footer (advanced; defaults to primary/secondary/tertiary props)

states.name: closed
states.description: open=false; backdrop and dialog are not rendered

states.name: open
states.description: Backdrop visible, dialog rendered, focus trapped inside

states.name: loading
states.description: Primary action shows a loading state; backdrop click and Escape are disabled until resolved

contentGuidelines: Title: short, action-oriented ("Delete tenant?" not "Confirmation"). Body: explain consequences, especially for destructive actions. Primary action label echoes the title's verb ("Delete"). Secondary action is always "Cancel" unless there's a specific alternative.

dos: Trap focus inside the dialog while open and restore focus on close
Use a clear, action-verbs title ("Delete tenant?" not "Are you sure?")
Echo the destructive verb in the primary button (Delete, Remove, Discard)
Disable Escape/backdrop close while a destructive primary action is in-flight
donts: Don't open a dialog from another dialog — restructure the flow into a single surface
Don't use a dialog for non-blocking notifications — use the Notification component
Don't put primary actions on the left of the footer
Don't autofocus a destructive primary action — autofocus the safest option (Cancel) instead

keyboard.action: Cycle focus inside the dialog (focus trap)
Closes the dialog when dismissible=true; emits close with reason='escape'

screenReader.announcement: [Title], dialog — focus moves to the first focusable element inside; backdrop is announced as a modal interruption
Focus returns to the element that opened the dialog; backgrounded content becomes interactive again

wcag.howMet: Uses role="dialog", aria-modal="true", aria-labelledby points at the title
Full keyboard support: Tab cycles within dialog, Escape closes, Enter activates primary
Focus is trapped inside the dialog while open and restored to the trigger on close — no keyboard trap escape required
On open, focus moves to the first focusable element (typically the close button or first form field); on close, focus returns to the opener
All interactive elements inside the dialog show a focus ring
Title, body, and action labels meet 4.5:1 contrast against the dialog surface

knownIssues: Stacked dialog support is intentionally out of scope for 0.2
visualHierarchy: Dialogs interrupt the page — use sparingly. The primary action sits bottom-right, secondary to its left, tertiary aligned to the bottom-left.
densityBehavior: Body padding adapts to density; default examples use comfortable density even when triggered from a compact toolbar.
_legacyAccessibilityNotes: Use role="dialog" with aria-modal="true" and an accessible name via aria-labelledby. Focus should move into the dialog on open and restore on close.
